gloss.en: the growth of something into a more advanced state

words.example: The town has seen rapid development in recent years.

gloss.en: a piece of equipment made to do a job

words.example: My phone is a very useful device.

gloss.en: great love or loyalty to someone or something

words.example: Her devotion to her family is clear.

gloss.en: a simple drawing that explains how something works

words.example: The teacher drew a diagram on the board.

gloss.en: the round face of a clock or machine with numbers

words.example: He looked at the dial on the old clock.

gloss.en: a conversation between people, especially in a story

words.example: The dialog in the movie felt very natural.

gloss.en: a conversation in a book, play, or film

words.example: Students practised the dialogue from the textbook.

gloss.en: a straight line across a circle through its centre

words.example: The circle has a diameter of ten centimetres.

gloss.en: a clear, very hard precious stone

words.example: She wore a beautiful diamond ring.

gloss.en: a soft cloth worn by a baby around the bottom

words.example: The baby needs a clean diaper.

gloss.en: a problem or hard situation

words.example: She had difficulty finding a good job.

gloss.en: using electronic signals shown as numbers

words.example: I bought a new digital camera.

gloss.en: careful and hard work

words.example: She passed the exam through hard work and diligence.

gloss.en: working hard and carefully

words.example: He is a diligent student who never gives up.

gloss.en: a compound with two oxygen atoms, as in carbon dioxide

words.example: Plants take in carbon dioxide from the air.

gloss.en: going straight without stopping or turning

words.example: We took a direct flight to Tokyo.

gloss.en: without stopping or changing direction

words.example: The path leads directly to the river.

gloss.en: dust, mud, or soil that makes things dirty

words.example: His clothes were covered in dirt.

gloss.en: to stop something from working

words.example: You can disable the alarm with this button.

gloss.en: something that causes problems or makes success harder

words.example: One disadvantage of the job is the long hours.

gloss.en: sad because something was not as good as hoped

words.example: I was disappointed by the film's weak ending.

gloss.en: not as good as you had hoped

words.example: The result was very disappointing for the team.

gloss.en: a very bad event that causes great damage

words.example: The flood was a terrible disaster for the town.

gloss.en: a slight pain or uneasy feeling

words.example: The medicine eased her discomfort.

gloss.en: the act of finding something new or unknown

words.example: Scientists made an important discovery this year.

gloss.en: an illness affecting people, animals, or plants

words.example: This disease can spread very quickly.

gloss.en: very unpleasant and making you feel sick

words.example: The smell in the room was disgusting.

gloss.en: a machine that washes dishes

words.example: Please put the plates in the dishwasher.

gloss.en: a feeling of not liking someone or something

words.example: He has a strong dislike of spicy food.

gloss.en: the amount of space between two places

words.example: The distance between the two towns is short.

gloss.en: clearly and in a way easy to notice

words.example: I distinctly remember locking the door.

gloss.en: something that interrupts or causes trouble

words.example: The loud party caused a disturbance at night.

gloss.en: a person who swims under water

words.example: The diver explored the coral reef.

gloss.en: the sport of jumping into or swimming under water

words.example: We went diving on our holiday by the sea.

gloss.en: no longer married after a legal end

words.example: My parents are divorced.

gloss.en: a person who plays music at a party or on radio

words.example: The dj played great songs all night.

gloss.en: an informal word for a doctor or a document

words.example: The doc told me to rest for a week.

gloss.en: a film or programme giving facts about something

words.example: We watched a documentary about wild animals.

gloss.en: a clever sea animal that looks like a large fish

words.example: We saw a dolphin jump out of the sea.

gloss.en: twice as much or as many

words.example: I ordered a double helping of rice.

gloss.en: a feeling of being unsure about something

words.example: There is no doubt that she will win.

gloss.en: on a lower floor of a building

words.example: The house has a downstairs bathroom.

gloss.en: moving or pointing toward a lower place

words.example: Prices showed a downward trend last month.

gloss.en: a short, light sleep

words.example: He had a quick doze on the sofa.

gloss.en: wearing clothes

words.example: Hurry up and get dressed.

gloss.en: a very small amount of liquid in a round shape

words.example: The first drop of rain fell on my hand.

gloss.en: a round musical instrument you hit to play

words.example: He learned to play the drum as a child.

gloss.en: having drunk too much alcohol

words.example: They were too drunk to drive home.

gloss.en: an informal word for a man or guy

words.example: Hey dude, are you coming to the party?

gloss.en: caused by or because of something

words.example: The delay was due to heavy traffic.

gloss.en: a fine powder of sand, earth, or dirt

words.example: There was dust all over the old books.

gloss.en: a container for rubbish kept outside

words.example: Please put the rubbish in the dustbin.

gloss.en: covered with dust

words.example: The old room was dark and dusty.

gloss.en: something you must do as a responsibility

words.example: It is my duty to help my parents.

gloss.en: sold without tax, usually at airports

words.example: We bought duty-free perfume at the airport.

gloss.en: a soft, thick cover for a bed

words.example: In winter I sleep under a warm duvet.

gloss.en: a series of rulers from the same family

words.example: The Tang dynasty ruled China for centuries.

gloss.en: a strong wish to do something

words.example: Her eagerness to learn impressed the teacher.

gloss.en: a pain inside your ear

words.example: The child cried because of a bad earache.

gloss.en: serious and sincere

words.example: She made an earnest promise to do better.

gloss.en: a sudden violent shaking of the ground

words.example: The earthquake destroyed many old houses.

gloss.en: in or from the east

words.example: They live in the eastern part of the country.

gloss.en: relaxed and not easily worried

words.example: My new boss is friendly and easy-going.

gloss.en: relaxed and not easily worried

words.example: He has an easygoing and calm personality.

gloss.en: relating to nature and the environment

words.example: The hotel is an eco resort by the forest.

gloss.en: the study of living things and their environment

words.example: She studies the ecology of the rainforest.

gloss.en: connected with trade, money, and wealth

words.example: They discussed the country's economic problems.

gloss.en: a country's system of money, trade, and industry

words.example: The economy is slowly growing again.

gloss.en: all the living things in an area and their environment

words.example: Pollution can harm the whole ecosystem.

gloss.en: a feeling of very great happiness

words.example: She was in ecstasy when she won the prize.

gloss.en: the outer limit of an object or area

words.example: He stood at the edge of the cliff.

gloss.en: a person who decides what goes in a paper or book

words.example: The editor checked every page of the report.

gloss.en: to teach someone over a long time

words.example: Schools educate children for the future.

gloss.en: connected with education or learning

words.example: The museum offers many educational programmes.

gloss.en: producing the result you want

words.example: This is a simple but effective treatment.

gloss.en: an attempt to do something difficult

words.example: She passed the test with a lot of effort.

gloss.en: choosing people for a position by voting

words.example: The election will be held next month.

gloss.en: connected with or using electricity

words.example: There was an electrical fault in the car.

gloss.en: energy used for light, heat, and machines

words.example: The storm cut off our electricity for hours.

gloss.en: a tiny particle with a negative electric charge

words.example: An electron carries a negative charge.

gloss.en: using small parts that control electric current

words.example: Please turn off all electronic devices.

gloss.en: a necessary or typical part of something

words.example: Trust is a key element of any friendship.

gloss.en: to make someone feel shy or uncomfortable

words.example: Please don't embarrass me in front of my friends.

gloss.en: feeling shy, uncomfortable, or ashamed

words.example: I felt embarrassed when I forgot her name.

gloss.en: making you feel shy or uncomfortable

words.example: It was an embarrassing moment for everyone.

gloss.en: a sudden dangerous event needing fast action

words.example: Call this number in an emergency.

gloss.en: a strong feeling such as love or fear

words.example: She tried to hide her emotion during the speech.

gloss.en: a man who rules an empire

words.example: The emperor ruled over a vast empire.

gloss.en: to give special importance to something

words.example: The teacher likes to emphasise the main points.

gloss.en: work done to earn money; having a job

words.example: He is looking for full-time employment.

gloss.en: with nothing or no one inside

words.example: The box was completely empty.

gloss.en: to surround something, or to put it inside

words.example: Please enclose a photo with your letter.

gloss.en: to give someone support, courage, or hope

words.example: My teacher always tries to encourage me.

gloss.en: a book or set giving facts on many subjects

words.example: I looked up the word in an encyclopaedia.

gloss.en: a book or set giving facts on many subjects

words.example: She read about whales in the encyclopedia.

gloss.en: to put someone or something in danger

words.example: Fast driving can endanger other people.

gloss.en: a person who hates or opposes another

words.example: He did not have a single enemy in the world.

gloss.en: having agreed to marry someone

words.example: They got engaged last summer.

gloss.en: the part of a vehicle that makes it move

words.example: The car engine would not start.

gloss.en: using science to design and build machines or roads

words.example: He studies engineering at university.

gloss.en: a man from England

words.example: The Englishman spoke with a London accent.

gloss.en: the feeling of pleasure from something

words.example: Music brings me a lot of enjoyment.

gloss.en: to amuse or please people

words.example: The clown tried to entertain the children.

gloss.en: a person who amuses an audience

words.example: The entertainer sang and danced on stage.

gloss.en: films, music, etc. used to amuse people

words.example: There is plenty of entertainment in the city.

gloss.en: a door or gate used to enter a place

words.example: We met at the front entrance of the hotel.

gloss.en: the act of going into a place

words.example: The sign said no entry to this room.

gloss.en: connected with the natural world we live in

words.example: We must reduce the environmental impact of waste.

gloss.en: a person who works to protect nature

words.example: The environmentalist spoke about saving forests.

gloss.en: one part of a TV or radio series

words.example: I watched the first episode of the show.

gloss.en: to the same degree or in the same way

words.example: Diet and exercise are equally important.

gloss.en: the things needed for an activity or purpose

words.example: We need new equipment for the kitchen.

gloss.en: the act of getting away from a place or danger

words.example: They made a quick escape from the fire.

gloss.en: completely necessary or very important

words.example: Water is essential for life.

gloss.en: time without end; a very long time

words.example: The wait felt like an eternity.

gloss.en: in an equal or balanced way

words.example: Spread the butter evenly on the bread.

gloss.en: in the end, after a long time

words.example: Our flight eventually left five hours late.

gloss.en: correct in every detail

words.example: Can you tell me the exact time?

gloss.en: to look at or study something carefully

words.example: The doctor will examine you now.

gloss.en: a person who tests students in an exam

words.example: The examiner asked me several questions.
